subject: Geography
title: The Youngest Borders
description: Learn The Youngest Borders in one minute.

hook.text: South Sudan became the world's newest country in 2011 after decades of civil war.

buildup.text: A referendum saw 98% vote for independence from Sudan.

discovery.text: Drawing the new border was agonizing—oil fields sat right on the line between the two nations.

twist.text: Within two years of independence, South Sudan erupted into its own civil war.

climax.text: Creating a new border solved one conflict and ignited another.

punchline.text: A new line on the map doesn't guarantee a new peace.

quiz.question: When did South Sudan become independent?
